name: Jonah
pattern: Called clearly to a hard place; flees the opposite direction; disciplined by storm and great fish; obeys outwardly but resents the mercy extended to those he was sent to.
failure_mode: Resentment that grace is given to enemies; preferring death to being part of mercy's instrument.
restoration_path: Name the running before God names it; let the heart-work continue past the obedience.
markers: anger when relief comes for those despised

name: Elijah Under the Juniper
pattern: After a great victory and great fear, the prophet collapses in self-pity, requests death, is fed by an angel, walks forty days, and is rebuked gently by a still small voice.
failure_mode: Conflating personal exhaustion with abandonment by God; assuming one is alone when seven thousand have not bowed.
restoration_path: Eat, sleep, walk, listen for the still small voice rather than the wind, earthquake, or fire.

name: The Rich Young Ruler
pattern: Approaches eagerly with the right question; loved by Christ; given the one specific instruction that touches the actual hold on him; walks away sorrowful.
failure_mode: Asking the right question but refusing the answer when it costs the thing one was secretly trusting.
restoration_path: Sell what holds you; the hold is rarely the obvious thing.
markers: one specific instruction touches the actual hold

name: Mary, Handmaid of the Lord
pattern: Receives an impossible calling, asks the necessary practical question, then yields with let-it-be; ponders things in her heart through years of unfolding.
failure_mode: None recorded; the model of consenting trust.
restoration_path: Yield, then ponder, then keep yielding through what you cannot yet see.

name: Samuel the Child
pattern: Hears his name in the night and runs to the wrong source three times; learns to answer Speak, for thy servant heareth; receives a hard word and reports it fully when asked.
failure_mode: Initially attributing God's voice to the nearest human authority.
restoration_path: Be still; say Speak; report what you heard even when it is hard.
markers: attributing it to the wrong source
mentor recognizes what young person cannot

name: Abraham at Moriah
pattern: Receives a command that contradicts the prior promise; obeys early in the morning; trusts the LORD will provide; ram caught in thicket; promise reaffirmed.
failure_mode: None at Moriah; earlier failure in Hagar shortcut.
restoration_path: Obey while you still hold the contradiction; let the LORD provide.
markers: carries the means of sacrifice himself

name: Peter at the Fire
pattern: Loud confidence in private; warms himself at the enemy's fire; denies three times; weeps bitterly; restored three times by the seaside with do you love me.
failure_mode: Confident bravado that collapses in proximity to power and accusation.
restoration_path: Be restored by the same number of askings as the failure; feed the sheep.

name: Thomas the Doubter
pattern: Honest about what he does not believe; wants tangible evidence; given it personally; confesses My Lord and my God.
failure_mode: Demanding terms of proof that exclude testimony of trusted witnesses.
restoration_path: State the doubt honestly; remain present with the brothers; receive when shown.

name: Judas Iscariot
pattern: Insider trusted with the bag; grievance over costly worship; sells the Lord for thirty pieces; remorse without repentance; suicide.
failure_mode: Remorse that returns the money but does not return to mercy.
restoration_path: Available until the rope; remorse must turn toward the cross, not away from it.

name: Ahithophel
pattern: Wise counselor whose counsel was as if a man inquired of God; defects to the usurper; counsel overturned by providence; hangs himself when ignored.
failure_mode: Treating one's wisdom as identity; cannot survive being unheard.
restoration_path: Wisdom held loosely; identity not in being followed.

name: Cain
pattern: Offering not accepted; countenance falls; warned that sin lies at the door and must be ruled; refuses warning; murders brother; carries the mark and the wandering.
failure_mode: Envy of a brother's accepted worship transmuted into violence rather than correction.
restoration_path: Rule sin at the door before it crouches further; bring the right offering.

name: Jacob Who Wrestled
pattern: The supplanter wrestles all night with the Man; will not let go without blessing; is renamed Israel; limps the rest of his life.
failure_mode: Earlier life as deceiver; striving to grasp what was promised.
restoration_path: Wrestle in the dark; ask the blessing; accept the limp as the proof.
markers: long history of striving and deceit
will not let go without blessing

name: Job
pattern: Blameless and upright; loses everything in a hidden contest; counselled wrongly by friends; refuses to curse; is answered out of the whirlwind not with reasons but with God himself; restored doubly.
failure_mode: None in the offering; in the dialogue, edges toward demanding God explain himself.
restoration_path: Lay the hand on the mouth; behold God; intercede for the accusers.

name: Habakkuk
pattern: Cries how long; receives an answer harder than the question; stands upon the watchtower; ends in trembling rejoicing though the fig tree shall not blossom.
failure_mode: None; the model of the watchful complaint.
restoration_path: Complain honestly; wait at the watchtower; rejoice though the fields are bare.
markers: rejoices though nothing visible has changed

name: Asaph
pattern: Almost slipped envying the prosperity of the wicked; until I went into the sanctuary of God; then I understood their end.
failure_mode: Envy of wicked prosperity until vantage shifts.
restoration_path: Enter the sanctuary; see the end.

name: Esther
pattern: Hidden Jewish queen in a foreign court; warned that her position is not exempt; called to such a time as this; fasts three days; risks the king's scepter; reveals her people; turns the decree.
failure_mode: Wanting position without its purpose.
restoration_path: Fast, risk, name the threat plainly, accept if I perish I perish.

name: Joseph in Egypt
pattern: Loved son betrayed by brothers; slave to Potiphar; resists adultery; forgotten in prison; raised to second in Egypt; forgives with you meant it for evil but God meant it for good.
failure_mode: Youthful boasting about the dream.
restoration_path: Serve faithfully wherever placed; refuse the easy sin; forgive when the brothers come.
markers: interprets meaning for those above him

name: Daniel
pattern: Carried into Babylon; purposes not to defile himself with the king's portion; opens his window toward Jerusalem three times daily; prays the corporate confession for a sin not his own; remains faithful through four kings.
failure_mode: None recorded.
restoration_path: Pre-decide the line; pray in public; confess corporately.

name: The Three in the Furnace
pattern: Confronted with worship-or-die; answer our God is able to deliver us, but if not, we will not bow; enter the furnace; the fourth man is with them; come out without the smell of smoke.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Speak the but-if-not; enter the fire; trust the Fourth Man.
markers: God may not rescue, will not bow

name: Shiphrah and Puah
pattern: Commanded by Pharaoh to kill male Hebrew infants; feared God rather than the king; lied to save lives; God dealt well with them and gave them houses.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Fear God over the king; preserve life even when commanded to take it.

name: Moses at the Bush
pattern: At the burning bush; offers five objections; given the rod, the sign of the hand, Aaron as mouthpiece; God's anger kindles at the fifth refusal; goes anyway.
failure_mode: Refusal-by-inadequacy taken too far.
restoration_path: Five objections is the limit; pick up the rod.

name: Gideon and the Fleece
pattern: Threshing in the winepress for fear; called mighty man of valor while hiding; tears down his father's altar at night; lays fleece twice; army reduced from thirty-two thousand to three hundred.
failure_mode: Fear of the men of his city; ephod made later becomes a snare.
restoration_path: Smash the family idol even at night; let God reduce the army; do not make an ephod after victory.

name: Jeremiah the Weeping Prophet
pattern: Pleads I am a child; ordained from the womb; preaches to a nation that will not listen; word becomes fire shut up in his bones; weeps for the daughter of his people.
failure_mode: None; the model of faithful unheeded prophecy.
restoration_path: Speak though no one listens; let the word burn rather than escape.
markers: word like fire in the bones

name: Saul Who Was Disqualified
pattern: Tall and chosen; offers the sacrifice himself when Samuel delays; spares Agag and the best livestock; consults a medium at the end; falls on his own sword.
failure_mode: Partial obedience reframed as the people made me do it; fear of man over fear of God.
restoration_path: Obey fully and early; do not justify partial obedience by spoils.

name: The Sons of Eli
pattern: Priests who knew not the LORD; took the best of the sacrifices by force; lay with the women at the tent door; father rebukes lightly; both die in one day, the ark captured.
failure_mode: Office without reverence; abuse of access to the sacred.
restoration_path: Reverence cannot be inherited; the office does not save the officer.

name: Demas Who Loved This World
pattern: Fellow worker named in earlier letters; in the last letter, Demas hath forsaken me, having loved this present world, and is departed unto Thessalonica.
failure_mode: Slow drift; the world's love wins over the long road.
restoration_path: Watch the slow drift; the present world is the danger, not just persecution.
markers: named for the leaving, not the joining

name: Saul Who Became Paul
pattern: Breathing threats and slaughter; struck blind on the road; named chief of sinners; counts former gains as loss; preaches the faith he sought to destroy.
failure_mode: Earlier zeal misdirected toward destroying the church.
restoration_path: Confronted on the road; rise, go, be told what to do.

name: Manasseh in Chains
pattern: Most wicked king of Judah; bound in chains and carried to Babylon; humbled himself greatly; sought the LORD his God; was brought back; reformed worship as far as he could.
failure_mode: Decades of idolatry, child sacrifice, occult.
restoration_path: Even the worst, when bound in chains, can be heard if they humble themselves greatly.
markers: reforms what he can in time remaining

name: Zacchaeus the Tax Collector
pattern: Wealthy chief publican; climbs the sycamore to see; Jesus calls him by name; restitution offered fourfold beyond the law's requirement; salvation today.
failure_mode: Wealth gained by complicity with the occupier.
restoration_path: Climb to see; receive when called; restore beyond the requirement.

name: The Prodigal Son
pattern: Demands the inheritance early; wastes it in a far country; comes to himself in the pigsty; rehearses a speech of unworthiness; the father runs while he is yet a great way off.
failure_mode: Treating the father as a means to inheritance.
restoration_path: Come to yourself; rise and go; the father runs.

name: The Elder Brother
pattern: Faithful in the field but resents the feast; will not go in; the father comes out to entreat him; cannot say my brother, says this thy son.
failure_mode: Service without sonship; bitterness at unearned mercy.
restoration_path: Receive that all the father has is yours; rejoice that the brother is alive.
markers: father comes out to him too

name: The Good Samaritan
pattern: The wounded man on the Jericho road; priest passes by; Levite passes by; the despised foreigner has compassion, binds, pays, and returns to settle the bill.
failure_mode: None for the Samaritan; failure modeled in the religious who passed.
restoration_path: Be the neighbor in deed at personal cost; return to settle what is owed.

name: The Pharisee and the Publican
pattern: Two go up to pray; one with himself recounts his virtues and despises the other; one beats his breast and says God be merciful to me a sinner; the second goes home justified.
failure_mode: Prayer that is a soliloquy of comparison.
restoration_path: Stand afar off; beat the breast; ask mercy.
markers: thanks God I am not like others

name: The Wise and Foolish Virgins
pattern: Ten virgins with lamps await the bridegroom; five carry oil in vessels, five do not; the bridegroom tarries; cry at midnight; the foolish ask to borrow but cannot; the door is shut.
failure_mode: Lamps without reserve; readiness presumed but not stored.
restoration_path: Carry oil in vessels with the lamps; readiness is provisioned, not improvised.
markers: oil cannot be borrowed at the end

name: The Servant Who Buried the Talent
pattern: Receives one talent; afraid of the master, hides it in the ground; returns exactly what was given; called wicked and slothful; the talent given to the one who already had ten.
failure_mode: Fear-driven preservation that produces no fruit; theological misread of the master.
restoration_path: At least give it to the exchangers; risk the gain over the buried preservation.
markers: named by the failure to multiply

name: Nicodemus by Night
pattern: Pharisee, ruler of the Jews; comes by night with respectful question; struggles with born again; later defends Jesus in the council; brings a hundred pound weight of spices to the burial.
failure_mode: Slow approach from inside the establishment.
restoration_path: Begin in the night; speak up in the council when you can; come fully at the burial.
markers: public costly act at the end

name: The Woman at the Well
pattern: Comes at the sixth hour to avoid the village women; engages a strange Jew; her past named without condemnation; leaves the waterpot, goes to the city, says come, see a man who told me all things that ever I did.
failure_mode: Serial broken covenants in relationship; village shame.
restoration_path: Be named without condemnation; leave the waterpot; tell the village.

name: The Centurion
pattern: Gentile officer; loves the nation, builds a synagogue; will not ask Jesus to enter under his roof; recognizes authority by analogy to his own; commended for faith not found in Israel.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Recognize authority where it is; receive at distance if that is what humility requires.

name: Isaiah, Here Am I
pattern: Sees the LORD high and lifted up; cries woe is me, I am undone; lip touched with the coal; hears whom shall I send; answers here am I, send me.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: See, confess undone, receive cleansing, then volunteer.

name: Caleb Who Wholly Followed
pattern: One of twelve spies; brings a minority good report; survives forty years of wandering for others' unbelief; at eighty-five asks for the mountain Hebron with its giants still there.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Hold the minority report; outlast the unbelief; claim the hard mountain in your eighties.
markers: asks for the hardest portion late in life

name: Melchizedek
pattern: King of Salem, priest of God Most High; brings out bread and wine; blesses Abraham; receives tithes; without recorded father, mother, or genealogy; remains a priest continually.
failure_mode: None; a type, not a fallible man.
restoration_path: N/A; pattern indicates a priesthood not by descent but by oath.

name: Isaac the Offered Son
pattern: Only son of promise; carries the wood himself; asks where is the lamb; does not resist on the altar; received back from the dead in figure.
failure_mode: None at Moriah.
restoration_path: N/A as type; pattern is the consenting son.
markers: carries the means of his death
received back as from the dead

name: David the Shepherd-King
pattern: Anointed while still in the field with the sheep; refuses Sauls armor for the smooth stones he knows; spares Saul twice in the caves; falls grievously with Bathsheba and Uriah; receives Nathans parable and writes Psalm 51 from inside the failure.
failure_mode: Power that exempts itself from the moral demands it enforces on others.
restoration_path: Receive the prophets word; repent in public; rebuild the broken bones rather than cover the bloodguilt.
markers: spares the king who hunts him
writes confession from inside the sin

name: Moses Who Said Blot Me Out
pattern: After the golden calf, ascends the mountain again and offers his own name to be blotted from the book if the people can be spared; later interposes again after the spies refusal; stays in the breach.
failure_mode: None at the intercession itself; earlier strikes the rock in anger.
restoration_path: Stand in the gap; offer self before God for the people; do not bargain from the safe side.
markers: positioned to be saved while others perish
appeals to Gods name not the peoples merit

name: Noah
pattern: Found grace in a generation otherwise lost; builds the ark by faith over decades while the world watches and mocks; is moved with fear and prepares; preserves a remnant; later vulnerable in the new beginning to wine and shame.
failure_mode: After the deliverance, the lapse in the tent.
restoration_path: Walk with God; build what you are told even when no rain has fallen; do not assume the new world is exempt from old weaknesses.

name: Nehemiah the Wall-Builder
pattern: Hears that Jerusalems walls are broken; weeps, fasts, prays, and asks the king; arrives, surveys at night, organizes by household; works with sword in one hand and trowel in the other; refuses to come down for distractions: I am doing a great work.
failure_mode: None during the wall-building; later harsh reform.
restoration_path: Weep before you plan; survey by night before you announce; refuse the meeting that pulls you off the work.
markers: builds with one hand on the sword
refuses to come down for distractions

name: Solomon the Wise-Then-Divided
pattern: Asks for wisdom and is given it abundantly; builds the temple; receives the queen of Sheba; multiplies wives and horses and gold against the law of the king; gods of his wives turn his heart; the kingdom torn after his death.
failure_mode: Wisdom that does not govern its own appetites; the very things forbidden to a king are multiplied.
restoration_path: Wisdom is given to be applied first to ones own house; the explicit prohibition is not negotiable for the gifted.
markers: asks for wisdom rather than wealth
gifted but turned away in old age

name: Hannah at the Tabernacle
pattern: Barren and provoked by the rival; weeps bitterly at the tabernacle, prays so silently her lips move but no voice; misjudged by Eli as drunk; vows the child to the LORD if given; receives Samuel and returns him; sings the song the Magnificat echoes.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Bring the bitter prayer to the right house; receive what is given; return it to the giver; sing.
markers: vows the gift back to the giver

name: Simeon Who Waited
pattern: Just and devout; waiting for the consolation of Israel; promised he would not see death before he saw the Christ; led by the Spirit to the temple at the right moment; receives the infant in his arms; releases his servant in peace.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Wait for the consolation; let the Spirit move you to the right room at the right hour; recognize the small thing that fulfills the great promise.
markers: recognizes the small in arms as the great
released in peace after the seeing

name: Anna the Prophetess
pattern: Widowed seven years after a brief marriage; serves God in the temple with fastings and prayers night and day for decades; arrives at that hour; gives thanks; speaks of Him to all who looked for redemption in Jerusalem.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Decades of unseen prayer are not waste; the moment of recognition comes; tell those who are also waiting.
markers: fasting and prayer night and day

name: Jonathan
pattern: Crown prince who loves David as his own soul; gives him his robe and sword; warns him of his fathers intent; finds him in the wood at Ziph and strengthens his hand in God; dies on Mount Gilboa with his father.
failure_mode: None toward David; dies in the lost cause of his fathers house.
restoration_path: Recognize the anointing on another even when it costs your inheritance; cross enemy lines to strengthen the friend in the wood.
markers: heir who recognizes the displacing anointing
gives his own robe to the displacer
warns the friend at personal risk
dies in his fathers failed cause

name: Ruth the Moabite
pattern: Refuses to leave the bitter widowed mother-in-law: thy people shall be my people, and thy God my God; gleans in the field of Boaz; spreads her skirt over the kinsman by night; is redeemed; becomes great-grandmother of David.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Bind yourself by oath to the people you have chosen; glean where the kinsman field happens to be; rest at the threshing-floor; receive the redemption.
markers: gleans humbly in the right field
approaches the kinsman with the right vulnerability

name: Boaz the Kinsman-Redeemer
pattern: Notices the foreign woman gleaning; protects her among the young men; lets her glean among the sheaves and pulls handfuls on purpose; recognizes her appeal at the threshing-floor; settles the redemption at the city gate before witnesses.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Protect the vulnerable in your field; settle the matter publicly at the gate.

name: Aaron and Hur
pattern: As Joshua fights below, Moses raised rod brings victory; when his hands grow heavy, Aaron and Hur set a stone under him to sit on and stay his hands, one on each side, until the going down of the sun; Amalek defeated.
failure_mode: None at the rock; Aaron later in the calf.
restoration_path: When the one whose lifted hands decide the day grows weary, stand under each arm; keep his hands steady until the sun goes down.
markers: hold up the arms each on a side
day is won not by them but through them

name: Mordecai at the Gate
pattern: Sits at the gate; uncovers the assassination plot; refuses to bow to Haman; condemned to the gallows Haman built; on a sleepless night the king reads the chronicles and Mordecai is honored; eventually elevated to second in the kingdom.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Sit at the gate faithfully; report what you hear; refuse the bow; let the sleepless night come.
markers: condemned by the very gallows enemy built
vindicated through the kings sleepless reading

name: Mephibosheth
pattern: Crippled in both feet from a fall when his nurse fled; hidden in Lo-debar (place of no pasture); summoned by David for Jonathans sake; eats at the kings table continually; later slandered by Ziba but receives Davids justice.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Be willing to be found in the place of no pasture; receive the kindness for the covenants sake; eat at the table.
markers: hidden in a place of no pasture

name: Nebuchadnezzar Who Was Humbled
pattern: Walking on the roof of his palace: is not this great Babylon, that I have built; voice from heaven, sanity removed, eats grass like an ox seven years; at the end of the days lifts up his eyes to heaven and understanding returns; praises the Most High.
failure_mode: Self-attribution of what was granted.
restoration_path: Lift your eyes to heaven; the Most High rules in the kingdoms of men.
markers: lifts eyes to heaven and recovers

name: Uzziah Who Reached for the Censer
pattern: Strong as long as he sought the LORD; when strong, his heart lifted up to destruction; enters the temple to burn incense which is the priests alone; opposed by Azariah and eighty priests; leprosy breaks out in his forehead at that moment; lives in a separate house leprous to the day of his death.
failure_mode: Crossing the line of ones office when strong.
restoration_path: Strength is the test that pride often fails; honor the boundary even when no one would stop you.
markers: reaches for an office not given

name: Balaam Son of Beor
pattern: Diviner the kings hire; cannot curse what God has blessed and says so when speaking; nevertheless counsels Balak to send the women of Moab to seduce Israel into Baal-peor; dies by the sword in that battle.
failure_mode: Speaking true prophecy while planning the back-door corruption.
restoration_path: None taken; the warning to those who would sell truth for wages.
markers: but loves the wages of unrighteousness
provides the workaround when direct curse fails
the donkey sees what the prophet does not
dies among those he counseled to corrupt

name: Jezebel
pattern: Tyrian princess; massacres the prophets of the LORD; sustains the four hundred prophets of Baal at her table; threatens Elijah; engineers Naboths judicial murder for a vineyard; paints her face at the end and is thrown down from the window.
failure_mode: Power that views truth-telling as treason and uses procedure to murder.
restoration_path: None recorded.
markers: engineers judicial murder via false witness
painted face at the end is the same face

name: Micaiah ben Imlah
pattern: Four hundred prophets unanimous before two kings; Micaiah summoned, warned to say what the others say; gives a sarcastic agreement; pressed under oath, prophesies the disaster honestly; sees Israel scattered as sheep without a shepherd; struck by Zedekiah, jailed on bread and water.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Speak what the LORD shows you even when four hundred are unanimous the other way; accept the cost.
markers: unanimous majority on the wrong side
sarcastic agreement first then truth under oath
struck and jailed for telling it

name: John the Baptist
pattern: The voice crying in the wilderness; calls the religious a generation of vipers; baptizes for repentance; recognizes Jesus and decreases that He may increase; rebukes Herod for taking his brothers wife; loses his head for the rebuke.
failure_mode: None; near the end sends from prison asking if Jesus is the one — honest doubt without abandonment.
restoration_path: Decrease that He may increase; rebuke the kings sin to his face; trust the One you announced even in the prison.
markers: honest doubt from prison without abandonment

name: Stephen the First Martyr
pattern: Full of faith and the Holy Spirit; face like an angel before the council; recounts Israels pattern of resisting the Spirit; sees the heavens opened and the Son of Man standing; prays as he is stoned: Lord, lay not this sin to their charge.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Speak truth to the council though it costs you; intercede for your killers as you fall.
markers: face like an angel before accusers

name: Lot in Sodom
pattern: Chooses the well-watered plain toward Sodom; pitches toward, then lives in; troubled in his righteous soul; lingers when warned to flee; rescued by the hand of mercy; loses wife to backward look; ends in a cave with daughters.
failure_mode: Gradual proximity to corruption that costs everything including the family.
restoration_path: Do not linger when warned; do not look back; the well-watered plain is not the same as the right place.
markers: mercy drags him out and he still loses much

name: Jephthahs Rash Vow
pattern: Outcast son who becomes a mighty warrior; before battle vows whatever first comes out of the doors of his house if victorious; daughter comes out to meet him with timbrels; tears his clothes but holds to the vow.
failure_mode: Bargaining with God in language one would not accept back; rigid keeping of an unrighteous oath.
restoration_path: Numbers 30 and Leviticus 27 both gave him a way out; he chose rigid pride over correction.
markers: mighty in battle, foolish at the threshold
vow with no thought to what might come
first to come out is the beloved
tears clothes but does not seek the way out

name: Josiah Who Found the Book
pattern: Eight years old when he begins to reign; in his eighteenth year while repairing the temple the book of the law is found; tears his clothes when it is read; gathers the people, reads it aloud, makes covenant; tears down the high places and the asherim; passes too like a comet.
failure_mode: None in the reform; later dies in a battle he insisted on joining against warning.
restoration_path: Tear your clothes when the book is read; do not patch the temple while leaving the high places.
markers: young king of a corrupted house
public covenant under the heard word

name: Ezra the Scribe
pattern: Prepared his heart to seek, do, and teach in Israel statutes and judgments; refuses a military escort because he had told the king the hand of God is upon them; on hearing of the foreign marriages tears his garment, plucks hair from his head and beard, sits astonished till the evening sacrifice; leads corporate confession; reform follows.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Seek, do, then teach — in that order; refuse the escort you would not have boasted in; sit astonished before you act.
markers: sits astonished till the evening sacrifice
leads corporate confession before structural change

name: Deborah Under the Palm
pattern: Judges Israel under the palm of Deborah; sends for Barak with the LORDs word; goes with him when he refuses to go alone, foretelling the honor will go to a woman; the Kishon sweeps Siseras chariots; Jael drives the tent peg; Deborah sings.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Sit where the people come; speak the word given; go with the reluctant if you must but say what the cost of his reluctance is.
markers: woman judge in a male era
goes with him at his price
Kishon and Jael close the matter

name: Gehazi the Servant
pattern: Servant of Elisha; sees Naaman healed and his refused thanks; runs after the chariot with a story about young prophets needing silver; takes two talents and changes clothes; Elisha sees in spirit; the leprosy of Naaman attaches to Gehazi.
failure_mode: Greed under the prophets roof; using the masters name for ones own pocket.
restoration_path: None taken in the text; the leprosy clings.
markers: sees the miracle and the refused payment
crafts a plausible story for the silver
what was refused for the master attaches to the servant

name: Naaman the Syrian
pattern: Commander of Syrias army, mighty and honorable, but a leper; little Israelite captive girl says the prophet could heal him; arrives with horses and chariots; offended at being told to dip seven times in the Jordan, expected ceremony; servants reason with him; dips, becomes as a child; refuses to take the diseases price elsewhere.
failure_mode: Earlier pride that almost refused the simple cure.
restoration_path: Listen to the captive girl; obey the simple word instead of waiting for the grand one; dip seven times.
markers: offended at first by the simplicity
obeys and becomes as a child

name: The Thief Who Asked to be Remembered
pattern: Justly condemned beside the cross; rebukes the other thief; admits his own due reward; turns to Jesus: Lord, remember me when thou comest into thy kingdom; receives today thou shalt be with me in paradise.
failure_mode: A life of wrongdoing ending here.
restoration_path: At the last breath, the right confession and the right address still admit one to paradise.
markers: addresses Jesus as Lord at the lowest hour

name: Cornelius the Centurion
pattern: Devout Gentile who feared God with all his house; gave much alms and prayed always; sees a vision telling him to send for Peter; calls his kinsmen and near friends to hear what Peter will say; Spirit falls on all of them; baptized.
failure_mode: None recorded.
restoration_path: Pray always, give alms; send for the word; gather your house to receive what you receive.
markers: alms and prayer accepted as memorial
gathers his house for the hearing
Spirit falls on all who hear

name: Onesiphorus Who Refreshed
pattern: Sought Paul out diligently in Rome and found him; was not ashamed of his chain; refreshed him often; ministered at Ephesus in many things.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Find the one in chains and refresh him; be unashamed when others are ashamed.
markers: also faithful at home in Ephesus
named by Paul among the named

name: Ananias and Sapphira
pattern: Sell a possession and keep back part of the price; bring the partial as though it were the whole; Peter discerns the lie is not to men but to the Holy Spirit; both fall dead in turn; great fear comes on the church.
failure_mode: Pretending to a generosity not actually given; lying to the Spirit while the church watches.
restoration_path: None taken.
markers: kept-back portion presented as the whole

name: Barnabas Son of Encouragement
pattern: Sells his field at the start; brings the new convert Saul to the apostles when they fear him; sent to confirm the work at Antioch; goes for Saul at Tarsus to bring him in; sticks up for John Mark after a falling-out and takes him on the second journey when Paul will not.
failure_mode: None.
restoration_path: Sell the field; vouch for the suspected convert; take a chance on the failed disciple again.
markers: stands for the failed disciple over the apostles objection

name: Apollos Corrected
pattern: Eloquent man, mighty in the scriptures, fervent in spirit; knows only the baptism of John; speaks boldly in the synagogue; Aquila and Priscilla take him aside and expound the way of God more perfectly; goes on to mightily convince.
failure_mode: Eloquent partial understanding before the correction.
restoration_path: Receive correction from less-public believers; let the way of God be expounded more perfectly to you.

name: Lazarus of Bethany
pattern: Sick to death; sisters send for Jesus, who tarries two days; arrives when Lazarus is four days in the tomb; weeps at the grave though He knew what He would do; cries Lazarus, come forth; the dead man comes out bound hand and foot.
failure_mode: None on Lazarus part.
restoration_path: N/A as type; the pattern is the called-by-name from the four-days-in-the-tomb death.
markers: sickness ends in death despite faith
four days, no hope by reckoning
called by name out of the tomb

name: The Centurion at the Cross
pattern: Roman officer overseeing the execution; sees how He cries out and gives up the spirit; sees the things that happened — darkness, earthquake, the tearing of the veil reported later; declares: Truly this was the Son of God.
failure_mode: His prior part in the execution; the soldiers around him casting lots and mocking.
restoration_path: What you watched die can be confessed at the moment of the death itself.
markers: confesses out loud at the cross
